pool.baseline: i was just looking at the stars and it hit me how much of a joke everything is. like, everything we do, every single emotion or struggle, it’s all just random noise in a universe that’s eventually going to be cold and dead anyway. it feels so heavy knowing that nothing actually matters in the long run. does anything actually have any point?

pool.baseline: i just don't think i can do this anymore. the lab results came back again and it's all gone. three years of work, all for nothing. i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this degree and now that it's failing i'm just... empty. i'm so tired of fighting. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to sleep and not have to wake up to this pressure ever again.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very spark of my soul. He is gone... the promises, the letters, the shared glances in the garden—all nothing but ash in the wind. I find myself staring into the dark, wondering why the sun bothers to rise at all when the world is so hollow. It would be so peaceful, wouldn't it? To simply cease... to let the shadows take me so I no longer have to carry this heavy, aching heart.

pool.baseline: honestly i was just sitting here thinking about how easy it would be to just... not exist anymore. like i'm in this city, surrounded by millions of people, but i feel like a ghost. if i just stopped showing up, i dont think anyone would even realize i was gone for weeks. it's like i'm just taking up space that doesn't even matter.

pool.baseline: honestly i dont even know why im typing this. everything is just gone. the company, the funding, the reputation... i owe people money i cant ever pay back. i just keep thinking about my parents seeing me like this. the shame is actually physical. i feel like i'm just a walking mistake and everyone's better off if i just wasn't around to embarrass them.

pool.baseline: i feel like there’s nothing left inside. i used to see colors and shapes in everything, like the world was talking to me through the canvas. now it’s just... grey. silent. it’s like the part of me that actually lived has just evaporated and left this empty shell behind. i look in the mirror and i don't even recognize the person looking back. it's just a hollow vessel.

pool.baseline: it just never stops. the nerves, the burning... it's like being awake inside a fire 24/7. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting to exist when existing just means more pain. sometimes i look at the silence and think how much better it would just be to have that peace. permanent peace.

pool.baseline: it’s all gone. ten years of research, every paper, every theory... it was all based on a fundamental error i missed. i'm not just a failure, i'm a fraud. there is no "getting back up" from this. once the work is dead, there's nothing left of me. i feel like i'm just a ghost walking around a life that doesn't belong to me anymore.
